sdkworkchat.name: SDKWORK Official Account
sdkworkchat.description: Use the built-in SDKWORK official account delivery path so OpenClaw can hand off conversations without extra credential setup.

setupSteps.0: Open the integrated SDKWORK official account experience or install the SDKWORK client if this machine has not signed in yet.
setupSteps.1: Sign in with your SDKWORK account so the default media channel is ready immediately.
setupSteps.2: Keep this channel enabled when the current runtime should hand off into the SDKWORK official account.

wehcat.name: WeChat Official Account
wehcat.description: Connect a WeChat official account workflow so OpenClaw can serve China-facing media channels.

setupSteps.0: Create or manage a WeChat official account in the WeChat official account platform.
setupSteps.1: Paste the App ID, App Secret, token, and optional AES key here.
setupSteps.2: Configure the callback URL on the WeChat side and enable the official account channel.

qq.name: QQ
qq.description: Connect a QQ bot so OpenClaw can route commands, alerts, and approvals into QQ groups.

setupSteps.0: Create or manage the target QQ bot in the QQ bot platform.
setupSteps.1: Paste the bot key and target group ID here.
setupSteps.2: Enable the channel after a dry-run delivery succeeds.

groupId.label: Group ID
groupId.helpText: The target QQ group that receives OpenClaw updates.

dingtalk.name: DingTalk
dingtalk.description: Connect a DingTalk custom robot so OpenClaw can broadcast updates into DingTalk workspaces.

setupSteps.0: Create a custom robot in the target DingTalk group.
setupSteps.1: Copy the access token and signing secret into this form.
setupSteps.2: Enable the channel after the first connectivity check succeeds.

wecom.name: WeCom
wecom.description: Connect a WeCom application so OpenClaw can serve enterprise WeCom conversations.

setupSteps.0: Create a WeCom application with bot or customer-contact permissions.
setupSteps.1: Paste the corp ID, agent ID, and secret here.
setupSteps.2: Save the configuration and verify that message delivery succeeds.

feishu.name: Feishu
feishu.description: Connect a Feishu bot so OpenClaw can receive and reply to team messages.

setupSteps.0: Create a Feishu app in the open platform.
setupSteps.1: Copy the App ID and App Secret into this form.
setupSteps.2: Add the event callback URL from your OpenClaw deployment if needed.

telegram.name: Telegram
telegram.description: Use a Telegram bot token to bring OpenClaw into direct messages or group chats.

setupSteps.0: Create a bot with BotFather and copy the bot token.
setupSteps.1: Optionally set a webhook URL if Telegram should push events to your host.
setupSteps.2: Enable the channel after the required credentials are filled.

errorPolicy.label: Error Policy
errorPolicy.helpText: Optional Telegram delivery error policy, for example retry or disable.

whatsapp.name: WhatsApp
whatsapp.description: Manage optional WhatsApp access rules for direct-message allowlists and group delivery behavior.

setupSteps.0: Authenticate the OpenClaw WhatsApp channel with the official CLI or runtime login flow.
setupSteps.1: Optionally restrict allowed direct-message senders or define per-group behavior here.
setupSteps.2: Keep the channel enabled so runtime login state can be reused without extra config wiring.

allowFrom.label: Allow From
allowFrom.helpText: Optional allowlist of direct-message senders. Enter one phone number per line or a JSON array.

groups.label: Groups
groups.helpText: Optional JSON object of WhatsApp group rules. Use "*" to define defaults for all groups.

discord.name: Discord
discord.description: Attach OpenClaw to a Discord bot for server and DM conversations.

setupSteps.0: Create a Discord application and bot in the developer portal.
setupSteps.1: Paste the bot token here and invite the bot to your server.
setupSteps.2: Turn the channel on once the token has been validated.

slack.name: Slack
slack.description: Configure bot and app tokens so OpenClaw can work inside Slack workspaces.

setupSteps.0: Create or open your Slack app and install it to the target workspace.
setupSteps.1: Paste the bot token and app token below.
setupSteps.2: Add a signing secret if your workspace uses slash commands or events.

googlechat.name: Google Chat
googlechat.description: Provide Google Chat service account or reference details for enterprise workspace delivery.

setupSteps.0: Create a Google Chat app and service account.
setupSteps.1: Provide either the inline service account JSON or a service account reference.
setupSteps.2: Fill audience or webhook information if your deployment requires it.

ironclaw.description: Security-first Rust-native assistant with database-backed memory and routines.

openclaw.recommendedReason: OpenClaw docs recommend onboarding-first setup, and Windows hosts are usually most stable through WSL2.
openclaw.firstRun: Finish provider setup, choose channels, and add starter skills before your first live workflow.

prerequisites.one: Node 24 is recommended. Node 22.16+ is still supported.
prerequisites.two: Windows should prefer WSL2 for the cleanest day-to-day runtime.
prerequisites.three: Docker is optional for isolated gateways, not the default install path.

zeroclaw.recommendedPath: Clone the repo and install with Cargo.
zeroclaw.recommendedReason: The install manifests and vendor docs both use the Rust source profile; the old pnpm alias is only a compatibility shim.
zeroclaw.firstRun: Confirm `zeroclaw --version` works, then continue with your own provider, channel, and hardware tuning.

prerequisites.one: Install Git plus the Rust toolchain with Cargo.
prerequisites.two: Windows builds should have Visual Studio Build Tools or an equivalent C++ toolchain.
prerequisites.three: ZeroClaw is optimized for tiny memory budgets and low-cost hardware.

ironclaw.recommendedPath: Clone the repo, build with Cargo, then run onboarding.
ironclaw.recommendedReason: IronClaw is source-first today and its README explicitly requires PostgreSQL + pgvector plus NEAR AI authentication.
ironclaw.firstRun: Create the database, enable pgvector, then run `ironclaw onboard` to finish auth and secret setup.

prerequisites.one: Install Git, Rust, and Cargo before building.
prerequisites.two: Prepare PostgreSQL + pgvector for memory and search features.
prerequisites.three: Keep NEAR AI credentials ready for the setup wizard.

empty.title: No scheduled tasks yet

scheduleModeHelp.interval: Run every selected number of minutes, hours, or days.
scheduleModeHelp.datetime: Run on one specific date and time.
scheduleModeHelp.cron: Use a raw 5- or 6-field cron expression for full control.

cards.noExecutionYet: This task has no execution history yet.

fields.enabledHelp: Disabled tasks are created paused so you can review them safely.
fields.promptPlaceholder: Describe what should happen every time this task runs...
fields.promptHelp: Keep the prompt outcome-focused so repeated runs stay reliable.
fields.cronTimezoneHelp: Optional IANA timezone. Leave empty to use the runtime default.
fields.staggerMsHelp: Optional deterministic spread window for recurring cron runs. Use 0 to keep exact timing.
fields.schedulePreviewPending: Complete the schedule inputs to generate a preview.
fields.cronPreviewPending: A cron preview will appear here when available.
fields.customSessionIdHelp: Persistent custom sessions keep context between scheduled runs.
fields.deleteAfterRunHelp: One-shot jobs can remove themselves automatically after a successful run.
fields.deliveryChannelHelp: Optional. Pick where summaries should be announced, or leave empty to reuse the last route.
fields.noConnectedChannels: No connected delivery channels are available yet.
fields.recipientHelp: Optional announce target such as a room, group, or contact.
fields.webhookUrlHelp: Use a valid HTTP(S) endpoint to receive finished cron event payloads.
fields.timeoutSecondsHelp: Stop overly long runs before they block later schedules.
fields.agentIdHelp: Pin this task to a specific OpenClaw agent when your runtime has multiple agents.
fields.agentIdDefaultHelp: Leave this on the default route to follow the connected instance's current default agent, now {{name}} ({{agentId}}).
fields.agentIdUnavailableHelp: This task is still pinned to {{agentId}}, but that agent is no longer available from the connected instance.
fields.agentIdCatalogHelp: Agent choices are read from the connected instance. Pick a specific agent only when this cron job must stay pinned to that agent.
fields.modelHelp: Optional provider/model or alias override for agent-turn tasks.
fields.thinkingHelp: Override the reasoning depth for supported GPT-5 and Codex models.
fields.lightContextHelp: Skip heavyweight workspace bootstrap when the task only needs lightweight context.
fields.toolAllowlistHelp: Enter one tool or group token per line. Leave blank to keep the upstream default tool policy.
fields.toolAllowlistTokensHelp: Accepts tool ids like exec or read, plus group:* shorthands such as group:filesystem.
fields.deliveryBestEffortHelp: Avoid failing the whole cron run when downstream delivery has a transient issue.
